title-group.article-title: RQM Coherence Framework v0.1: Spectral-Variational Coherence on S^3 x R

abstract.p: This paper introduces the first formal version of an RQM coherence framework on the manifold M = S^3 x R_s. The framework defines coherence as an operator-theoretic property relative to a native spectral-variational model, rather than as descriptive language. The state space is L^2(M) and the governing operator is A = -Delta_{S^3} - partial_s^2 + U(s), with local quadratic specialization U(s) = kappa (s - s_*)^2, kappa > 0. In this model, separated modes combine hyperspherical harmonics on S^3 = SU(2) with Hermite-Gaussian slice modes on R. The central coherence object is the residual functional R(Psi) = inf_E ||A Psi - E Psi||, which is zero for exact eigenforms and small for near-coherent states. Auxiliary measures are introduced for operator alignment, axis alignment, family concentration, and spectral concentration. A dissipative flow partial_t Psi = -A Psi is used to formalize temporal equilibration toward lower-energy coherent sectors.

license.license-p: This draft is distributed under the terms of the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0).

sec.title: 1. Introduction
sec.p: The paper formalizes coherence for Resonant Quantum Mechanics as an operator-theoretic relation on a concrete manifold and Hilbert space. It introduces a central residual object that distinguishes exact eigenform coherence from approximate and non-coherent states.

sec.title: 2. From descriptive coherence to operator coherence
sec.p: Coherence is defined relative to a self-adjoint operator A. Exact coherence is the eigenrelation A Psi = E Psi. Near-coherence is treated through a residual norm criterion.

sec.title: 3. The manifold S^3 x R
sec.p: The manifold is M = S^3 x R_s, where S^3 is identified with SU(2) as orientation manifold and s is a slice coordinate. The state space is L^2(S^3 x R).

sec.title: 4. Governing operator and separated spectrum
sec.p: The governing operator is A = -Delta_{S^3} - partial_s^2 + U(s), specialized to U(s) = kappa (s - s_*)^2. Separation yields orientation eigenvalues lambda_l = l(l+2) and slice eigenvalues mu_n = (2n+1)sqrt(kappa), with energies E_{l,n} = lambda_l + mu_n.

sec.title: 5. Residual coherence and auxiliary coherence measures
sec.p: The central residual functional is R(Psi) = inf_E ||A Psi - E Psi||. Additional measures include operator coherence, axis coherence, family coherence, and spectral concentration over selected mode sectors.

sec.title: 6. Temporal equilibration and preferred coherent families
sec.p: For the dissipative flow partial_t Psi = -A Psi, modal amplitudes decay as exp(-E_{l,n} t), so higher-energy sectors are suppressed faster than lower-energy sectors.

sec.title: 7. Why this framework matters for RQM Technologies
sec.p: The framework provides a shared metric backbone for coherence scoring, residual minimization, mode concentration tracking, and convergence diagnostics across RQM Studio, WaveEngine, and future coherence-locked systems.

sec.title: 8. Limits of the v0.1 model and next steps
sec.p: The paper does not claim complete physical derivation of all RQM phenomena or full empirical validation across products. It presents a formal candidate backbone for future algorithmic and engineering development.

sec.title: 9. Conclusion
sec.p: The framework elevates coherence from descriptive language to an operator-theoretic, variational, and falsifiable object on S^3 x R.

ref.mixed-citation: Hall, Brian C. Lie Groups, Lie Algebras, and Representations: An Elementary Introduction. Springer; 2015.
Chavel, Isaac. Eigenvalues in Riemannian Geometry. Academic Press; 1984.
Sakurai, J. J.; Napolitano, Jim. Modern Quantum Mechanics. Cambridge University Press; 2017.
Reed, Michael; Simon, Barry. Methods of Modern Mathematical Physics IV: Analysis of Operators. Academic Press; 1978.
